payload.title: Polish WHY.md into a public-facing essay (eval numbers + neighbors)
payload.description:
WHY.md was published in RT-38 as a rationale doc, but it was written before the eval numbers existed and before cross-provider validation. Polish into a publication-ready essay.

Tasks:
- Add a 'The numbers' section near the top with the 3-vendor table (Haiku / GPT-4o / Gemini 2.5 Flash, vanilla vs trust ship rates). Link runs 005 and 006.
- Add a 'Compared to neighbors' section: Sorbet (Ruby strict-mode lints), ReasonML / OCaml (syntax extensions over a base language), Mypy (gradual typing), Flow→TS history (typed dialect that won out over time). Short — 2-3 sentences each. Show Trust is a familiar pattern, not a moonshot.
- Audit existing text for claims that are now overstated/understated given the eval. e.g. 'we believe' lines that can be hardened to 'evals show'; speculative effects-system content that was dropped in RT-10.
- Trim. WHY.md as published is dense. The target audience is skeptical-but-curious — 8-12 minutes to read, not 25.

Done when: a Rust-aware developer can read it cover-to-cover and decide whether the idea is worth a try. Ready to link from the README and the launch posts.
